content.problemStatement:
Despite my ghoulish reputation, I really have the heart of a small boy. I keep it in a jar on my desk.

Alice wants to give Bob some goodies, but she's uncertain about how many to give.

To help determine the quantity, she presented Bob with the following challenge:

Alice has a tree, i.e, a simple connected undirected graph with NNN vertices and NŌłÆ1N-1NŌłÆ1 edges.

Each edge has a lowercase character written on it.

Alice also has a string SSS of length MMM, consisting of lowercase characters.

Define str(u,v)\text{str}(u, v)str(u,v) to be the string formed by the characters seen while traversing the edges on the unique path from uuu to vvv in the tree.

Alice asks Bob to choose two vertices uuu and vvv, and will give him LCS(str(u,v),S)\text{LCS}(\text{str}(u, v), S)LCS(str(u,v),S) goodies.

Here, LCS(A,B)\text{LCS}(A, B)LCS(A,B) denotes the length of the longest common **subsequence** of strings AAA and BBB.

For example, LCS("aba", "cd")=0,LCS("aba", "aa")=2\text{LCS}(\texttt{"aba", "cd"}) = 0, \text{LCS}(\texttt{"aba", "aa"}) = 2LCS("aba", "cd")=0,LCS("aba", "aa")=2, and LCS("abc", "cba")=1\text{LCS}(\texttt{"abc", "cba"}) = 1LCS("abc", "cba")=1.

If Bob chooses uuu and vvv optimally, what's the maximum number of goodies he can receive?


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN ŌĆö the number of vertices.
The next NŌłÆ1N - 1NŌłÆ1 lines describe the edges. The iii-th of these NŌłÆ1N - 1NŌłÆ1 lines contains two space-separated integers uiu_iuiŌĆŗ and viv_iviŌĆŗ, denoting an edge between uiu_iuiŌĆŗ and viv_iviŌĆŗ and a character cic_iciŌĆŗ written on the edge.
The final line contains Alice's string SSS, consisting of lowercase letters.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the maximum number of goodies Bob can get.
constraints: The input graph is a tree on NNN vertices.
cic_iciŌĆŗ is a lowercase Latin letter, i.e, between `'a'` and `'z`'.
The sum of NNN across all test cases won't exceed 10410^4104.
The sum of ŌłŻSŌłŻ|S|ŌłŻSŌłŻ across all test cases won't exceed 10310^3103.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The tree looks like this:

Bob can select u=1u = 1u=1 and v=6v = 6v=6.
The uŌåÆvu \to vuŌåÆv path is 1ŌåÆ2ŌåÆ3ŌåÆ61 \to 2 \to 3 \to 61ŌåÆ2ŌåÆ3ŌåÆ6, which results in the string "acd"\texttt{"acd"}"acd".
LCS("acd",┬Ā"abcd")=3\text{LCS}(\texttt{"acd", "abcd"}) = 3LCS("acd",┬Ā"abcd")=3, which is the best Bob can do.
Test case 222: The tree looks like this:

Bob can choose u=3u = 3u=3 and v=4v = 4v=4 to obtain the string "cd"\texttt{"cd"}"cd", whose LCS with S="cfgd"S = \texttt{"cfgd"}S="cfgd" is 222.
